danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile sprites in combat-ready states, armed soldiers and cyborg enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or combat engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Large alien/mutant entity sprites, slimy mutant appearance, cyborg forms
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-culminating danger; heightened threat level requiring full engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Unknown substance shown in cutscenes (cocaine equivalent in JP version, 'stuff' in US version)
danger_signals.meaning: Narrative danger signal; corrupting alien substance driving conspiracy

danger_signals.visual_form: Deformed human figures in flasks, transformed allies (Christy as slimy mutant)
danger_signals.meaning: Horror/corruption signal; loss of humanity and identity

danger_signals.visual_form: Waterfront construction sites, sewers, power plants, warehouse spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Contaminated/controlled spaces suggesting criminal activity and alien control

safety_signals.visual_form: Meat and consumables dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Player recovery resource availability indicates manageable threat moment

safety_signals.visual_form: Bullets and grenade supplies dropped from defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability signals enemy defeat and temporary power increase
Safety margin expansion; player resilience increase

safety_signals.visual_form: Christy and Sophia in cutscenes, Hart's support network
safety_signals.meaning: Allied support and narrative progression; world still has trustworthy elements

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corporate headquarters as final stage; corporate aesthetic in bio-tech laboratory and power plant
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed corporate mercenaries, cyborgs, cloned soldiers, mutated experiments, flying cyborg (former Captain Reese)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bio-Tech Laboratory, Corporate Headquarters, Power Plant, Warehouse 0001 - high-tech industrial spaces showing alien control infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien substance 'Gel'; monster forms and mutant creatures as visual representation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Large alien entities, slimy mutants, boss monsters showing non-human morphology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bio-Tech Laboratory with human experimentation flasks; scattered throughout B.E.D.A. controlled territories

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sorcerer Kim Long as faction leader
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mafia soldiers, criminal enforcers, monsters serving drug trade
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Chinatown, Port, Waterfront - urban criminal districts; intermediary distribution network

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Detective Hart and agent Christy as emblematic figures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Investigation-driven narrative; Hart's partner Reese initially presumed dead, reveals corruption within

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Normal urban and industrial environments initially; B.E.D.A. appears as ordinary corporation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard criminal investigation framework; enemies appear as hired mercenaries and thugs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environments shift from urban to research/experimental (jungle base, bio-lab); mutant and cyborg enemies increase
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy variety expands; allies reveal hidden agendas; narrative cutscenes show escalating threat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Hart's partner Reese revealed as corrupted
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cyborg transformation visual; military base aesthetic in Ricardo Range
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Betrayal moment; shift from trust to realization of infiltration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Human form transforms into slimy mutant creature in bio-laboratory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Emotional climax; loss of support network; confesses love before death

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Truth of Hart's nature (he is a clone)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final stage reveals Hart clone infrastructure; aged Hart villain as visual confirmation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Identity crisis resolution; final confrontation becomes personal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: BEDA Corporate Headquarters as final fortress; director appears as aged clone of Hart
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Director explains clone dynasty succession plan; Hart refuses offer; final monster transformation battle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Another clone emerges from flask after Hart's victory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Suggests defeat of conspiracy is temporary; alien/corporate control persists despite Hart's victory

environmental_storytelling.example: Route 246 (road investigation) → Waterfront/Chinatown (criminal underworld) → Ricardo Range (military base) → Bio-Tech Laboratory (alien experiments) → Corporate Headquarters (final truth)
Urban crime zones (mafia control) vs. military/research facilities (B.E.D.A. control) vs. jungle base (alien/mercenary hybrid operation)
Clean urban spaces early → gradual introduction of industrial contamination → bio-laboratory human experimentation → final corporate fortress revealing full alien control infrastructure
Movie-style sequences between stages reveal plot progression; each stage cutscene delivers conspiracy revelations through dialogue and visual transformation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy and ally appearance as trust indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Reese appears as war hero in memory → revealed as cyborg/corrupted → Christy appears as supportive ally → revealed as mutated victim

environmental_storytelling.technique: Vehicle segments as action variation tied to narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Road/driving stages (Stage 1, Stage 10) represent pursuit and escape mechanics tied to investigation progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: First-person shooting segments as perspective shift
environmental_storytelling.example: Stages 4-2 and 11-1 shift to Hart's direct perspective; gunplay segments represent defensive moments against overwhelming forces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Platforming through environments, collecting weapons/supplies; responding to environmental hazards
Vehicle-based combat; three-gear shifting; shooting from moving car platform
Final boss battle as human then monster form; refuses clone dynasty offer; continues fighting despite existential revelation
Provides investigation updates; analyzes substance; directs Hart to locations; cutscene presence as emotional anchor
Reveals as mutated creature in bio-laboratory; confesses love; dies providing final mission clue
Presumed dead; appears as flying cyborg boss; attempts to warn Hart before assassination
Boss enemy; controls mafia operations; linked to Gel substance distribution
Large sprite entities requiring sustained combat; each represents faction/conspiracy layer (truck monster, flying cyborg, mutant creature, aged Hart clone)
Visual representation of persistence; final clone activation after Hart's victory suggests cycle continuation
Standard platformer enemy patterns; drop weapon/health supplies upon defeat; indicate faction presence through environmental positioning

notes: Vice: Project Doom uses a multi-stage narrative structure (11 stages with cutscenes) to progressively reveal a layered conspiracy: street-level crime → organized crime networks → military corruption → alien infiltration → clone dynasty. Visual manifestation progresses from mundane urban environments to increasingly bizarre mutant/cyborg enemies and experimental facilities. The game communicates danger through enemy type escalation and environmental context shift. Regional censorship differences (Japan's explicit cocaine reference vs. US 'stuff'; Reese headshot vs. explosion) affected narrative tone but not mechanical world-state communication. Hart's journey from detective investigating substance to discovering his own clone nature parallels visual transition from noir/police procedural to biopunk sci-fi. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on stage theming and cutscene exposition rather than subtle environmental design.
